excerpt: Safety and security - Costa Rica travel advice - GOV.UK Cookies on GOV.UK We use some essential cookies to make this website work. Accept additional cookies Reject additional cookies View cookies Hide cookie message Skip to main content Foreign travel advice Costa Rica Subscriptions Get email alerts Safety and security Terrorism There is a high threat of terrorist attack globally affecting UK interests and British nationals, including from groups and individuals who view the UK and British nationals as targets. Find out how to reduce your risk from terrorism while abroad . Crime Protecting your belongings Muggings and theft are significant problems, particularly on the Atlantic coast. Violent crime Avoid walking alone in poorly lit or remote areas at night. number of foreign nationals including one British national have gone missing in recent years, with some incidents related to criminal activity. Criminals sometimes cause deliberate collisions to stop vehicles and commit robberies or other crimes. Spiked drinks and sexual assault Rapes and sexual assaults have increased, some of which appear targeted toward tourists.
